feedback.text: It is not clear to me, as a customer/final consumer, how it may be right if I have paid the full price, the manufacturer makes it absolutely impossible for me to play the game purchased. Online in which I have invested money (to get ‘speciales’) and, even to give my part to keep my part, are simply stopped, even if it had been absolutely OK for me as a user, if there were no updates above any new missions. Only 2 examples “Marvel Avengers Alliance” and “Need for speed world”. For these games, it would not have been technical – so I am 100 % sure – either to create an offline variant or to keep servers running instead of losing ALLES for a small fee. It is costly to keep servers in operation if – in my opinion – either it should be made clear at the outset what you will play here at some point, OR it will be possible for the player to restart on hardware that is currently accessible. Emulators should also be protected and NOT criminalised. Gaming firms should be obliged to provide a customer with an opportunity to maintain the game even after ‘housing’. If it is a multiplayer, please then allow it to play online on private servers if it is a single player game, then there can be no longer an ‘online check’ to be able to start the game. If the game contains “sale-items” (purchased with real money), a refund or credit’s are useful. It is simply unfair if someone has finally found a game that they passionately play for years, simply because of ‘win-optimisation’, they are given the opportunity to continue (even limited) to play/use. Game consoles have now been put on the market via ‘digital’ versions only, a game full price 80 the ‘Deluxe Edition’ ± 200, even if operator x decides after 2 years to stop it because it no longer reaches its turnover. That is not right. Makes gaming manufacturers obliged to ensure ‘customer-fair’ sustainability if they have already opted for a game.
